text: Two sessions per week of continuous running at maximal lactate steady state velocity (vMLSS) improved vMLSS by 8% over a training period in moderately trained runners.
Two sessions per week of intermittent running alternating 3-min repetitions 0.5 km/h above and below vMLSS improved vMLSS by 5% in moderately trained runners.
Continuous running at vMLSS twice per week increased VO2max by 10% in moderately trained runners.
Intermittent running around vMLSS twice per week increased VO2max by 6% in moderately trained runners.

Continuous running at vMLSS improved lactate threshold running speed by 7%, while intermittent running around vMLSS improved it by 9%, suggesting both modalities effectively raise the lactate threshold.
As few as two threshold-intensity sessions per week are sufficient to elicit significant improvements in lactate threshold, maximal lactate steady state, and VO2max in moderately trained runners.
Continuous steady-state running at threshold produced numerically larger improvements in vMLSS and VO2max than intermittent threshold running, though both were effective.
